title: Network Cables (UTP/STP/Coax)
description: Copper-based cables that carry electrical signals representing network data.
fullExplanation: Twisted pair cables use pairs of copper wires twisted together to cancel electromagnetic interference (EMI). The twisting causes noise picked up by both wires to cancel out (common-mode rejection). UTP (Unshielded Twisted Pair) is the most common. STP (Shielded Twisted Pair) adds a foil shield for noisy environments. Coaxial cable has a central conductor surrounded by insulation and a braided shield — used in cable TV and older Ethernet (10BASE2).
keyFacts: Maximum segment length: 100m for most categories
T568A and T568B are the two wiring standards
Auto-MDIX eliminates need for crossover cables
PoE (Power over Ethernet) delivers up to 90W over Cat5e+
Plenum-rated cables required in air ducts (fire safety)
